Towering sea-stacks pierce cobalt water beneath storm-torn skies, their ridged surfaces streaked white with gull colonies, waves exploding into foam against barnacle-crusted bases.
Natural stone arch frames churning emerald swells, water thundering through the opening as salt spray catches golden sunset light, eroded honeycomb patterns covering limestone walls.
Layered sedimentary cliffs drop vertically into jade-green water, each stratum a different ochre band, exposed by millennia of wave erosion under gathering purple thunderheads.
Hexagonal basalt columns descend like organ pipes into turquoise shallows, their geometric precision stark against wild Atlantic breakers crashing white across black volcanic rock.
Big Sur's jagged granite plunges into blue-green Pacific depths, kelp forests visible through crystalline water, morning fog rolling through coastal redwoods above rust-colored cliffs.
Oregon sea-stacks host barking sea lions, their dark forms clustered on barnacle platforms while grey waves surge through rock channels beneath overcast pewter skies.

Amalfi limestone cliffs plunge into impossibly turquoise Mediterranean water, sunlight illuminating underwater rock faces through crystal depths, whitewashed villages clinging impossibly to vertical stone.
Cornish granite cliffs meet roaring Atlantic surf, slate-grey waves twenty feet high smashing against dark rock under racing storm clouds, spray rising like ghosts.
Reynisdrangar's black basalt pillars rise from churning grey ocean, their jagged peaks silhouetted against dawn's pink light, volcanic sand beach stretching dark beneath towering cliff walls.
Cliffs of Moher drop seven hundred feet into white-capped Atlantic swells, layers of shale and sandstone exposed, storm light breaking through clouds in dramatic silver shafts.
Faroe Islands sea-cliffs draped in luminous moss cascade into steel-blue Arctic water, waterfall streams plummeting directly into surf, fog wreathing the upper ramparts in ghostly white.
Norwegian fjord mouth opens dramatically to midnight-sun ocean, granite walls glowing amber, glassy navy water reflecting sheer cliffs descending a thousand feet into glacial depths below.
Pacific Northwest tidal shelves reveal layered geology at low water, emerald pools trapped between barnacle ridges, purple starfish clinging to stone as silver mist drifts offshore.
California kelp beds undulate beneath crystal turquoise water, anchor chains of golden-brown fronds swaying against submerged boulders, sea otters floating among the translucent forest canopy.

Algarve limestone caves glow amber-gold inside wave-carved grottos, aquamarine water surging through natural tunnels, honeycomb erosion patterns covering cathedral-like chamber walls in afternoon sun.
Icelandic Dyrhólaey arch frames midnight-blue ocean through its massive volcanic portal, black sand beach curving below, puffin colonies dotting grass-topped cliffs under aurora-green twilight skies.
Orkney sandstone cliffs host screaming kittiwake colonies on narrow ledges, russet rock striped with white guano, jade-green water churning below under fast-moving cloud shadows and sudden sunlight.

Azores volcanic crater forms sheltered cobalt cove, jagged black lava cliffs encircling the protected basin, tropical azure water contrasting starkly with dark stone under dramatic towering cumulus clouds.

Patagonian cliffs meet furious South Atlantic breakers, wind-sculpted grey stone slick with constant spray, slate-blue water stretching to horizon beneath racing clouds streaming eastward toward Antarctica.

Basque coast receives Atlantic fury against iron-dark cliffs, white water exploding fifty feet upward through blowholes, gunmetal swells rolling in beneath bruised purple storm front advancing inland.

Tasmanian dolerite columns form geometric buttresses plunging into sapphire Southern Ocean, their hexagonal tops weathered flat, kelp-stained bases battered by jade swells under clearing squall light.

Madeira's volcanic ramparts rise sheer from indigo Atlantic depths, waterfalls vaporizing into mist before reaching wave-cut platforms below, laurel forests clinging to fractured basalt under golden afternoon sun.
Normandy chalk cliffs glow bone-white above bottle-green Channel waters, flint bands striping the pale stone, tide pools reflecting pewter skies as breakers foam across wave-cut limestone benches.
Galápagos lava flows frozen mid-plunge into cerulean Pacific, sea iguanas dotting black igneous shelves, turquoise water surging through collapsed lava tubes under equatorial sun blaze.

Nova Scotia lighthouse crowns rust-streaked granite headland above thrashing grey Atlantic, fog horns moaning through salt spray, dark spruce silhouettes backing cliffs where breakers detonate in white chaos.

Sicilian grottos carved deep into golden limestone reveal underwater galleries lit electric blue, Mediterranean swells surging into cathedral chambers, stalactites reflected in aquamarine shallows at cave mouths.

Kamchatka volcanic coast presents jagged obsidian teeth to steel-grey Pacific rollers, fumaroles venting steam among tide pools, snow-patched peaks behind cliffs where Steller's sea eagles perch on lava spires.

Cape of Good Hope's Table Mountain sandstone meets two-ocean convergence, Indian-Atlantic swells colliding in confused jade-grey peaks, Cape fur seals hauled on kelp-draped boulders under racing cloud shadows.
Cornish Tintagel ruins cling to slate promontory bisected by narrow gorge, pewter waves funneling through the gap, medieval walls crumbling into surf under brooding charcoal skies and wheeling gulls.

Chilean Patagonian fjord reveals layered metamorphic stone in copper and grey bands, glacial meltwater turquoise at surface fading to navy depths, condors soaring past thousand-foot walls under Magellanic wind.

Scottish sea-loch narrows dramatically between lichen-covered gneiss walls, black water mirror-calm reflecting heather slopes, single shaft of sunlight piercing Atlantic squall clouds illuminating the stone throat.
Namibian Skeleton Coast fog rolls over rust-orange dunes meeting grey breakers, seal colony barking on guano-white rocks, cold Benguela current churning jade through scattered shipwreck ribs.
Greek Santorini caldera rim drops vertically into volcanic crater-bay, white-cube houses perched impossibly on burgundy-black lava cliffs, ultramarine water filling the ancient eruption basin below.

Cinque Terre ochre and terracotta villages cling to terraced cliffs above cobalt Ligurian sea, ancient stone retaining walls cascading downward, vineyard-green slopes meeting azure water in impossible verticality.

Madagascar tsingy limestone needles meet Indian Ocean in serrated grey points, razor-sharp karst formations rising from turquoise shallows, mangrove roots threading through fossilized coral at the stone-forest edge.

Preikestolen granite precipice cleaves six hundred meters above Norwegian fjord's bottle-green depths, squared-off platform edge stark against vertical drop, hikers silhouetted tiny against void and distant waterfall veils.

Costa Brava pine-crowned coves shelter secret beaches between rust-streaked metamorphic headlands, crystalline aquamarine water lapping honey-colored sand, Mediterranean sun igniting wave-polished stone in amber light.

Moeraki spherical boulders scatter across dawn-grey New Zealand beach, septarian concretions cracked like turtle shells, lavender sunrise reflecting in tide pools between the geological mysteries' weathered forms.

New Zealand volcanic black sand meets turquoise Tasman breakers at Piha, massive Lion Rock sea-stack dominating the shore, rainforest-clad Waitakere cliffs backing the iron-dark beach under mist-wreathed heights.

Algarve Ponta da Piedade reveals maze of golden limestone pillars and tunnels, afternoon sun penetrating emerald grottos, tour boats threading natural arches where swallows nest in honeycomb erosion chambers above.
Shetland gneiss buttresses plunge into peat-dark Atlantic, ancient metamorphic swirls visible in wet stone faces, white gannets diving into chocolate-brown water stained by moorland runoff above.
Bretagne pink granite chaos tumbles into sapphire Bay of Biscay, rounded boulders stacked impossibly high by winter storms, tide pools glowing turquoise between lichen-painted rose-colored megaliths.
Pembrokeshire carboniferous limestone folded vertically creates jagged fin-walls in navy Celtic water, grey seal heads bobbing between tilted strata plates under fast-moving mackerel sky.

Lofoten Islands gneiss fangs pierce Arctic mirror-water, eagle nests wedged in vertical cracks, midnight sun casting tangerine glow across thousand-meter walls dropping into crystalline emerald fjord.
Donegal quartzite cliffs shimmer silver-white above hammered pewter Atlantic, horizontal bedding planes creating natural staircase for razorbills, spindrift horizontal in gale-force westerly wind.

Heard Island basalt columns meet subantarctic jade rollers, glaciers calving directly into surf behind volcanic pillars, albatross wheeling through sleet squalls above kelp-brown Southern Ocean swells.

Cinque Terre unnamed stack rises solitary from lapis Mediterranean, fisherman chapel ruins crowning the eroded pinnacle, swifts circling the isolated tower while sunset ignites wave-smoothed conglomerate gold.
Antrim basalt dyke cuts black through white chalk headland into bottle-green Irish Sea, geological collision frozen mid-argument, fulmars nesting where igneous intrusion meets sedimentary host rock.

Calanques limestone cuts sharp cream-colored fjords into Provence coastline, cobalt water filling drowned river valleys, rock climbers tiny on overhanging walls above glass-clear turquoise Mediterranean depths.

Westfjords basalt layer-cake cliffs host Arctic fox dens between strata, indigo water at cliff base,云breaking through storm reveals individual lava flows stacked like geological library under platinum light.
